title: Script R4: Virome Taxonomy
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"73vj8","text":"This protocol outlines our bacteriophage/virus taxonomy analyses. This analysis includes profiles for the average order relative abundance for each site, the phage species relative abundance profiles for each patient, and the relative abundance of specific species between sites. Intermediate files are also included with the publication, and the paths are specified below. Based on the methods from the following publication:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"671hf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7egnf","text":"Hannigan, Geoffrey D., et al. \"The Human Skin Double-Stranded DNA Virome: Topographical and Temporal Diversity, Genetic Enrichment, and Dynamic Associations with the Host Microbiome.\" mBio 6.5 (2015): e01578-15.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":184,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3hd3f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6vc9e","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

locale: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"del2t","text":"## [1] en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/C/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6qibd","text":"## ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9q0qm","text":"## attached base packages: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"88ro8","text":"## [1] stats graphics grDevices utils datasets methods base ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bsf74","text":"## ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d53ph","text":"## loaded via a namespace (and not attached): ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"eklnc","text":"## [1] magrittr_1.5    formatR_1.2    tools_3.2.0    htmltools_0.2.6 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"il9e","text":"## [5] yaml_2.1.13    stringi_0.4-1    rmarkdown_0.7    knitr_1.10.5 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"487q9","text":"## [9] stringr_1.0.0    digest_0.6.8    evaluate_0.7","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Load the required R packages. library(vegan)packageVersion( library(vegan) packageVersion("vegan") library(ggplot2) packageVersion("ggplot2") library(pgirmess) packageVersion("pgirmess") library(plyr) packageVersion("plyr") library(reshape2) packageVersion("reshape2") ## Loading required package: permute ## Loading required package: lattice ## This is vegan 2.3-0 ## [1] '2.3.0' ## [1] '1.0.1' ## [1] '1.6.0' ## [1] '1.8.2' ## [1] '1.4.1'

Import the data tables for order relative abundance. INPUT_ORDER INPUT_ORDER <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Phage_Taxonomy/order_rel_abund.tsv", header=FALSE, sep="\t") INPUT_ORDER[c(1:5), ] %s %s ## V1 V2 V3 ## 1 No_hit 99626.40000 MG100098 ## 2 Unclassified_Order 5596.65000 MG100098 ## 3 Caudovirales 11968.50000 MG100098 ## 4 Herpesvirales 7.61005 MG100098 ## 5 Mononegavirales 49.08570 MG100098

Import the data tables for species relative abundance. INPUT_SPECIES INPUT_SPECIES <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Phage_Taxonomy/species_rel_abund.tsv", header=FALSE, sep="\t") INPUT_SPECIES[c(1:5), ] %s %s ## V1 V2 V3 ## 1 Achromobacter_phage 0.00000 MG100098 ## 3 Human_immunodeficiency_virus 1.08351 MG100098 ## 4 Silicibacter_phage 0.00000 MG100098 ## 5 Clavibacter_phage 0.00000 MG100098 Serratia_phage 0.00000 MG100098

Input the mapping file. INPUT_MAP INPUT_MAP <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Mapping_files/SkinMet_and_Virome_001_metadata.tsv", header=TRUE) INPUT_MAP[c(1:4), c(1:5)] %s %s ## NexteraXT_SampleID NexteraXT_RunName NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID ## 1 MG100151 NexteraXT_007 MG100102 ## 2 MG100150 NexteraXT_007 MG100101 ## 3 MG100149 NexteraXT_007 <NA> ## 4 MG100146 NexteraXT_007 MG100098 ## NexteraXT_Virome_RunName SubjectID ## 1 NexteraXT_005 1 ## 2 NexteraXT_005 1 ## 3 <NA> 1 ## 4 NexteraXT_005 1

Here we need to reformat the mapping files. This means only looking at the two time points for which we have a complete data set (we have only partial data for time point 1), as well as excluding the sites and subjects for which we only have partial sampling (c(“Ba”,“Ph”,“Vf”,“Neg”)). SUBSET_MAP SUBSET_MAP <- INPUT_MAP[which(INPUT_MAP$TimePoint %in% c(2,3)), ] SUBSET_MAP <- SUBSET_MAP[-which(SUBSET_MAP$Site_Symbol %in% c("Ba","Ph","Vf","Neg")), ] SUBSET_MAP <- SUBSET_MAP[-which(SUBSET_MAP$SubjectID %in% c(2,3,9,11)), ] SUBSET_MAP <- SUBSET_MAP[-which(SUBSET_MAP$NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID %in% NA), ]

Now we can easily plot the viral and phage order relative abundance information by skin site. Get a vector of the sample IDs that we want to pull out for analysis. KEEP_SAMPLES KEEP_SAMPLES <- as.vector(SUBSET_MAP$NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID) INPUT_SUBSET <- INPUT_ORDER[which(INPUT_ORDER$V3 %in% c(KEEP_SAMPLES)), ]

For this analysis we are removing those contigs which did not have a hit. We are only look at those contigs with phage/virus hits. INPUT_SUBSET INPUT_SUBSET <- INPUT_SUBSET[-which(INPUT_SUBSET$V1 %in% c("No_hit")), ] INPUT_MERGE <- merge(INPUT_SUBSET, SUBSET_MAP, by.x="V3", by.y="NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID")
These variables are called species but they are only named this. They really contain the order information. SPECIES_MEAN

Also filter out those taxa that account for less than 0.5% of the mean relative abundance. MeanForExclusion 0.5),]" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="A46606FD46A1446AB6E432ECE1963B88" > MeanForExclusion <- ddply(SPECIES_MEAN, c("V1"), summarise, mean = mean(mean)) MeanForExclusion$Percent <- 100 * MeanForExclusion$mean / sum(MeanForExclusion$mean) MeanForExclusionCut <- MeanForExclusion[c(MeanForExclusion$Percent > 0.5),]
Now only use the filtered taxa. SpeciesMeanFiltered

Take a look at the data frame. head(SpeciesMeanFiltered) %s %s ## Site_Symbol V1 mean ## 1 Ac Caudovirales 6804.984 ## 4 Ac Multiple 3488.321 ## 6 Ac Unclassified_Order 1544.165 ## 7 Ac Caudovirales 8729.835 ## 10 Ac Multiple 3893.010 ## 12 Ac Unclassified_Order 2434.145
Plot the results. ggplot(SpeciesMeanFiltered, aes(x=Site_Symbol, y=mean, group=V1, fill=V1)) + theme_bw() + geom_bar(stat=

Now that we have looked at the information for taxonomic order, we also want to look at the species level. Here we are only going to look at the top 10 taxa, and include the remaining taxa relative abundance information as “Other”. #Same formatting and parsing as aboveKEEP_SAMPLES #Same formatting and parsing as above KEEP_SAMPLES <- as.vector(SUBSET_MAP$NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID) INPUT_SUBSET <- INPUT_SPECIES[which(INPUT_SPECIES$V3 %in% c(KEEP_SAMPLES)), ] INPUT_SUBSET <- INPUT_SUBSET[-which(INPUT_SUBSET$V1 %in% c("No_hit")), ] INPUT_MERGE <- merge(INPUT_SUBSET, SUBSET_MAP, by.x="V3", by.y="NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID") #* SPECIES_MEAN <- ddply(INPUT_MERGE, c("Site_Symbol","V1"), summarise, mean = mean(V2))

Get the top ten taxa so we can specifically look at them. TOP_TEN_MEAN TOP_TEN_MEAN <- ddply(SPECIES_MEAN, c("V1"), summarise, mean = mean(mean)) TOP_TEN_ORDER <- TOP_TEN_MEAN[c(order(TOP_TEN_MEAN$mean, decreasing=TRUE)),] TOP_TEN <- TOP_TEN_ORDER[c(1:10),] KEEP_TOP_TEN <- as.vector(TOP_TEN$V1) #* FINAL_TOP_TEN <- INPUT_MERGE[which(INPUT_MERGE$V1 %in% c(KEEP_TOP_TEN)), ] FINAL_OTHER <- INPUT_MERGE[-which(INPUT_MERGE$V1 %in% c(KEEP_TOP_TEN)), ] FINAL_OTHER$V3 <- factor(FINAL_OTHER$V3)

Get the rest of the relative abundance taxa into the "other" category. FINAL_OTHER_SUM FINAL_OTHER_SUM <- data.frame(tapply(FINAL_OTHER$V2, INDEX=list(FINAL_OTHER$V3), FUN=sum)) FINAL_OTHER_SUM$SampleID <- c(row.names(FINAL_OTHER_SUM)) colnames(FINAL_OTHER_SUM) <- c("V2", "V3") FINAL_OTHER_SUM$V2 <- as.numeric(as.character(FINAL_OTHER_SUM$V2)) FINAL_OTHER_SUM$V1 <- "Other" FINAL_OTHER_SUM_FORMAT <- FINAL_OTHER_SUM[,c(2,3,1)] FINAL_OTHER_MERGE <- merge(FINAL_OTHER_SUM_FORMAT, SUBSET_MAP, by.x="V3", by.y="NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID") TOTAL_FINAL <- rbind(FINAL_TOP_TEN, FINAL_OTHER_MERGE) head(TOTAL_FINAL)[,c(1:4)] %s %s ## V3 V1 V2 NexteraXT_SampleID ## 21 MG100195 Multiple 6.39256e+03 MG100171 ## 49 MG100195 Mycobacterium_phage 1.31141e+02 MG100171 ## 57 MG100195 Propionibacterium_phage 1.69708e+00 MG100171 ## 81 MG100195 Pseudomonas_phage 1.16539e+05 MG100171 ## 85 MG100195 Streptococcus_phage 1.36360e+02 MG100171 ## 87 MG100195 Human_papillomavirus 1.89501e+03 MG100171

Order according to relative abundance. TOP10_WITH_OTHER TOP10_WITH_OTHER <- ddply(TOTAL_FINAL, c("V1"), summarise, mean=mean(V2)) TOP10_WITH_OTHER <- TOP10_WITH_OTHER[!c(TOP10_WITH_OTHER$V1=="Other"),] TOP10_WITH_OTHER <- TOP10_WITH_OTHER[order(TOP10_WITH_OTHER$mean, decreasing=TRUE), ] ORDER_MEAN_NAMES_WITH_OTHER <- as.vector(TOP10_WITH_OTHER$V1)

Append other to the vector. ORDER_MEAN_NAMES_WITH_OTHER ORDER_MEAN_NAMES_WITH_OTHER <- c(ORDER_MEAN_NAMES_WITH_OTHER, "Other") TOTAL_FINAL$V1 <- factor(TOTAL_FINAL$V1, levels=c(ORDER_MEAN_NAMES_WITH_OTHER))

Get plotting of species by patient for graphing. The colors and other minor cosmetics can be fixed in illustrator. ggplot(TOTAL_FINAL, aes(x=factor(SubjectID), y=V2, fill=V1, order=V1)) + theme_bw() + geom_bar(stat=

We are also interested in some specific viral species relative abundances by site, after looking at the general subject profiles above. Here we look at the specific relative abundances of HPV, propionibacterium phages, and staphylococcus phages. First format the data. Get a list of the sample names. SAMPLE_NAMES SAMPLE_NAMES <- as.vector(unique(INPUT_SUBSET$V3)) IN_SUBSET_REL_ABUND <- data.frame(lapply(SAMPLE_NAMES, function(i) { SUBSET <- INPUT_SUBSET[c(INPUT_SUBSET$V3==i),] SUM <- sum(SUBSET$V2) SUBSET$Rel_Abund <- 100 * SUBSET$V2 / SUM colnames(SUBSET) <- c("Taxa","Hits","Sample",i) return(SUBSET) })) IN_SUBSET_REL_ABUND_SUB <- IN_SUBSET_REL_ABUND[,c("Taxa",SAMPLE_NAMES)] REL_ABUND_MELT <- melt(IN_SUBSET_REL_ABUND_SUB)

First we looked at the relative abundances of Staphylococcus phages. This is written as a set of copy/pasted script sections for taxonomic group. STAPH_REL_ABUND STAPH_REL_ABUND <- REL_ABUND_MELT[c(REL_ABUND_MELT$Taxa=="Staphylococcus_phage"), ] STAPH_MERGE <- merge(STAPH_REL_ABUND, SUBSET_MAP, by.x="variable", by.y="NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID")

Plot the data by occlusion status. ggplot(STAPH_MERGE, aes(x=Occlusion, y=value)) + theme_bw() + geom_boxplot(notch=TRUE) + scale_y_log10() + ggtitle( ## Warning in loop_apply(n, do.ply): Removed 1 rows containing non-finite values (stat_boxplot).

Perform stats using the kruskalmc subroutine. STAPH_MERGE$Occlusion STAPH_MERGE$Occlusion <- factor(STAPH_MERGE$Occlusion) kruskalmc(STAPH_MERGE$value, STAPH_MERGE$Occlusion) ## Multiple comparison test after Kruskal-Wallis ## p.value: 0.05 ## Comparisons %s %s obs.dif critical.dif difference Exposed-Intermittently_Occluded 10.76401 36.09383 FALSE Exposed-Occluded 44.70092 23.71043 TRUE Intermittently_Occulded-Occluded 55.46493 34.98437 TRUE

Plot by site category. ggplot(STAPH_MERGE, aes(x=Site_Categories, y=value)) + theme_bw() + geom_boxplot(notch=TRUE) + scale_y_log10() + ggtitle( ## Warning in loop_apply(n, do.ply): Removed 1 rows containing non-finite values (stat_boxplot).

Run the stats. STAPH_MERGE$Site_Categories STAPH_MERGE$Site_Categories <- factor(STAPH_MERGE$Site_Categories) kruskalmc(STAPH_MERGE$value, STAPH_MERGE$Site_Categories) ## Multiple comparison test after Kruskal-Wallis ## p.value: 0.05 ## Comparisons %s %s obs.dif critical.dif difference Intermittently_Moist-Moist 51.958476 28.54913 TRUE Intermittently_Moist-Sebaceous 53.065524 28.43069 TRUE Moist-Sebaceous 1.107048 25.32023 FALSE

Get the Propionibacerium phage rows. PROP_REL_ABUND PROP_REL_ABUND <- REL_ABUND_MELT[c(REL_ABUND_MELT$Taxa=="Propionibacterium_phage"), ] PROP_MERGE <- merge(PROP_REL_ABUND, SUBSET_MAP, by.x="variable", by.y="NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID")

Plot by occlusion status. ggplot(PROP_MERGE, aes(x=Occlusion, y=value)) + theme_bw() + geom_boxplot(notch=TRUE) + scale_y_log10() + ggtitle( ## Warning in loop_apply(n, do.ply): Removed 27 rows containing non-finite values (stat_boxplot).

Run the stats. PROP_MERGE$Occlusion PROP_MERGE$Occlusion <- factor(PROP_MERGE$Occlusion) kruskalmc(PROP_MERGE$value, PROP_MERGE$Occlusion) ## Multiple comparison test after Kruskal-Wallis ## p.value: 0.05 ## Comparisons %s %s obs.dif critical.dif difference Exposed-Intermittently_Occluded 31.54397 36.09383 FALSE Exposed-Occluded 20.64971 23.71043 FALSE Intermittently_Occluded-Occluded 10.89427 34.98437 FALSE

Plot by site category. ggplot(PROP_MERGE, aes(x=Site_Categories, y=value)) + theme_bw() + geom_boxplot(notch=TRUE) + scale_y_log10() + ggtitle( ## Warning in loop_apply(n, do.ply): Removed 27 rows containing non-finite values (stat_boxplot).

Run the stats. PROP_MERGE$Site_Categories PROP_MERGE$Site_Categories <- factor(PROP_MERGE$Site_Categories) kruskalmc(PROP_MERGE$value, PROP_MERGE$Site_Categories) %s %s obs.dif critical.dif difference Intermittently_Moist-Moist 10.71380 28.54913 FALSE Intermittently_Moist-Sebaceous 44.36156 28.43069 TRUE Moist-Sebaceous 55.07535 25.32023 TRUE

Finally do the same analysis for HPV. HPV_REL_ABUND HPV_REL_ABUND <- REL_ABUND_MELT[c(REL_ABUND_MELT$Taxa=="Human_papillomavirus"), ] HPV_MERGE <- merge(HPV_REL_ABUND, SUBSET_MAP, by.x="variable", by.y="NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID")

Plot by occlusion status. ggplot(HPV_MERGE, aes(x=Occlusion, y=value)) + theme_bw() + geom_boxplot(notch=TRUE) + scale_y_log10() + ggtitle( ## Warning in loop_apply(n, do.ply): Removed 56 rows containing non-finite values (stat_boxplot).

Run the stats. HPV_MERGE$Occlusion HPV_MERGE$Occlusion <- factor(HPV_MERGE$Occlusion) kruskalmc(HPV_MERGE$value, HPV_MERGE$Occlusion) %s %s obs.dif critical.dif difference Exposed-Intermittently_Occluded 58.94431 36.09383 TRUE Exposed-Occluded 33.67678 23.71043 TRUE Intermittently_Occulded-Occluded 25.26754 34.98437 FALSE

Plot by site category. ggplot(HPV_MERGE, aes(x=Site_Categories, y=value)) + theme_bw() + geom_boxplot(notch=TRUE) + scale_y_log10() + ggtitle( ## Warning in loop_apply(n, do.ply): Removed 56 rows containing non-finite values (stat_boxplot).

Run the stats. HPV_MERGE$Site_Categories HPV_MERGE$Site_Categories <- factor(HPV_MERGE$Site_Categories) kruskalmc(HPV_MERGE$value, HPV_MERGE$Site_Categories) ## Multiple comparison test after Kruskal-Wallis ## p.value: 0.05 ## Comparisons %s %s obs.dif critical.dif difference Intermittently_Moist-Moist 22.01579 28.54913 FALSE Intermittently_Moist-Sebaceous 36.03696 28.43069 TRUE Moist-Sebaceous 58.05275 25.32023 TRUE
